highlights.title: Traffic-Aware Randomized Smoothing for LLM-Based Network Intrusion Detection
highlights.summary: Traffic-Aware Randomized Smoothing (TA-RS) enhances LLM-based intrusion detection
highlights.detail: TA-RS injects Gaussian noise into controllable features during fine-tuning and certification, improving certified accuracy. Standard randomized smoothing yields weak certified accuracy without noise-augmented fine-tuning.

highlights.title: TRACE: Turn-level Reward Assignment via Credit Estimation for Long-Horizon Agents
highlights.summary: TRACE assigns rewards in multi-turn agents via credit estimation
highlights.detail: TRACE represents rollouts as state transitions and obtains log-probabilities from a frozen reference model. This approach addresses the challenge of credit assignment in multi-turn agents.

highlights.title: The Dynamic Verifiable Multi-Agent Human Agentic Loyalty Loop (DVM-HALL) Model and the Net Human-Agent Score (NHAS) in Autonomous Commerce
highlights.summary: DVM-HALL model reevaluates customer loyalty in autonomous commerce
highlights.detail: The DVM-HALL model synthesizes literature on human-machine teaming, consumer decision-making, and algorithmic trust dynamics to address the limitations of traditional loyalty models.

highlights.title: Inkling – Open-Weights 975B Parameter LLM
highlights.detail: Inkling is a large language model with open weights, allowing for further research and development.

highlights.title: Fair on the Surface: Transaction-Ordering Bias and MEV in Mysticeti DAG-based BFT Protocol
highlights.summary: Mysticeti DAG-based BFT protocol has transaction-ordering bias
highlights.detail: The bias can be exploited to profit at other users' expense, highlighting a problem with maximal extractable value (MEV).

items.summary: Traffic-Aware Randomized Smoothing (TA-RS) enhances LLM-based intrusion detection
items.detail: TA-RS injects Gaussian noise into controllable features during fine-tuning and certification, improving certified accuracy. Standard randomized smoothing yields weak certified accuracy without noise-augmented fine-tuning.

items.summary: TRACE assigns rewards in multi-turn agents via credit estimation
items.detail: TRACE represents rollouts as state transitions and obtains log-probabilities from a frozen reference model. This approach addresses the challenge of credit assignment in multi-turn agents.

items.summary: DVM-HALL model reevaluates customer loyalty in autonomous commerce
items.detail: The DVM-HALL model synthesizes literature on human-machine teaming, consumer decision-making, and algorithmic trust dynamics to address the limitations of traditional loyalty models.

items.title: Inkling – Open-Weights 975B Parameter LLM
items.summary: Inkling is a 975B parameter LLM
items.detail: Inkling is a large language model with open weights, allowing for further research and development.

items.title: Exploring Hierarchical Interest Representation For Meta Ads Deep Funnel Optimization
items.summary: Meta explores Hierarchical Interest Representation for ads
items.detail: Hierarchical Interest Representation aims to learn unified embeddings that connect users' interests with advertisers' offerings, enhancing ads optimization.

items.title: GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
items.summary: GPT-Red improves AI safety via self-play
items.detail: GPT-Red is an automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness.

items.title: Jul 6, 2026 Case Study Government of Alberta uses Claude to find and fix cybersecurity vulnerabilities across government systems
items.summary: Government of Alberta uses Claude for cybersecurity
items.detail: The Government of Alberta utilized Claude to identify and fix cybersecurity vulnerabilities across their systems.

items.title: Jul 9, 2026 Announcements Ben Bernanke appointed to Anthropic’s Long-Term Benefit Trust
items.summary: Ben Bernanke joins Anthropic's Long-Term Benefit Trust
items.detail: Ben Bernanke's appointment to Anthropic's Long-Term Benefit Trust reflects the company's commitment to long-term decision-making and benefit.

items.title: Jul 9, 2026 Announcements Introducing a way to reflect on how you use Claude
items.summary: Anthropic introduces reflection feature for Claude
items.detail: The new feature allows users to reflect on their usage of Claude, promoting more effective and responsible interaction with the AI system.

items.title: The US is advancing AI safety through state and federal action
items.summary: US advances AI safety through state and federal action
items.detail: OpenAI outlines a 'reverse federalism' approach to AI governance, where state laws contribute to a national framework for safe and democratic AI.

items.title: RISC-V Is Inevitable: State of the Union Keynote Argues
items.summary: RISC-V is considered inevitable
items.detail: The State of the Union Keynote argues that RISC-V is an inevitable development in the tech industry.

items.title: Many old shim versions are still accepted by secure boot
items.summary: Vulnerable shim versions bypass Secure Boot
items.detail: The CMU CERT Coordination Center advisory highlights that many exploitable shim versions were not added to the revocation list, allowing attackers to bypass Secure Boot protections.

items.title: Security updates for Wednesday
items.summary: Security updates issued by AlmaLinux, Debian, Fedora, Mageia, Oracle, Red Hat, SUSE
items.detail: The updates address various vulnerabilities in multiple packages, including kernel, openssl, and python3.

items.title: [$] Lockless MPSC FIFO queues for io_uring
items.summary: io_uring uses lockless MPSC FIFO queues in 7.2 kernel
items.detail: The new queue improves performance and is relatively approachable, showing how lockless algorithms can work.

items.title: Local DoS attack vectors in seunshare 3.10 (SUSE Security Team Blog)
items.summary: Local DoS attack vectors found in seunshare 3.10
items.detail: The vulnerabilities can be exploited to achieve privilege escalation on SELinux-enabled systems.

items.title: [$] LWN.net Weekly Edition for July 16, 2026
items.summary: LWN.net Weekly Edition for July 16, 2026
items.detail: The edition covers various topics, including scraper bots, io_uring queues, and Debian bookworm.

items.title: Fair on the Surface: Transaction-Ordering Bias and MEV in Mysticeti DAG-based BFT Protocol
items.summary: Mysticeti DAG-based BFT protocol has transaction-ordering bias
items.detail: The bias can be exploited to profit at other users' expense, highlighting a problem with maximal extractable value (MEV).

items.title: A Distributed Framework for Compiling and Reasoning with d-DNNF
items.summary: Distributed framework for compiling d-DNNF
items.detail: The framework, called dkc, effectively partitions the search space into independent sub-problems.

items.title: From Continuous Deployment to Queryable Dataset: Terabyte-Scale AIS-Aligned Passive Acoustic Labelling
items.summary: Terabyte-scale AIS-aligned passive acoustic labelling
items.detail: The approach uses a database-native workflow to align hydrophone recordings with AIS position reports.

items.title: From Interpretation to Compilation: Compilation-Based Execution of Semantic Operators [Vision]
items.summary: Compilation-based execution of semantic operators
items.detail: The approach translates semantic operator specifications into deterministic executable code.

items.title: Backpropagation for Effectful Languages I: Finite Probability and Discrete Output Algebraic Effects
items.summary: Backpropagation for effectful languages
items.detail: The construction is formulated in the framework of Combinatory Homomorphic Automatic Differentiation (CHAD).

items.title: Lexicographic Direct Access with Functional Dependencies
items.summary: Lexicographic direct access with functional dependencies
items.detail: The approach gives fine-grained lower and upper bounds on the preprocessing time required.

items.title: facebookincubator/velox
items.summary: Velox: a composable C++ execution engine library
items.detail: The library is designed for data management systems and is fully extensible.

items.title: fmtlib/fmt
items.summary: Fmt: a modern C++ formatting library
items.detail: The library provides a simple and efficient way to format strings.

items.title: opencv/opencv
items.summary: OpenCV is an open-source computer vision library.
items.detail: It provides a wide range of functions for image and video processing, feature detection, and object recognition. OpenCV is widely used in various fields, including robotics, surveillance, and medical imaging.

items.title: tensorflow/tensorflow
items.summary: TensorFlow is an open-source machine learning framework.
items.detail: It supports a wide range of algorithms and is used in various applications, including natural language processing, computer vision, and speech recognition. TensorFlow is widely adopted in the industry and academia.

items.title: ArthurSonzogni/FTXUI
items.summary: FTXUI is a C++ functional terminal user interface library.
items.detail: It provides a simple and easy-to-use API for building terminal-based user interfaces. FTXUI is suitable for building command-line tools and applications.

items.title: BoundaryML/baml
items.summary: BAML is a programming language for agents.
items.detail: It is designed to support the development of autonomous agents and provides features such as concurrency and communication primitives. BAML is implemented in Rust.
